title: Marked by False Gods
description:
Edward Lee got fired this morning for falling asleep at his desk.

He was still lying on his bed thinking about rent when a ball of light the size of a tennis ball floated into his room and told him he had been selected as a constellation manager.

"Selected by who?"

[Permission too low for the question to be answered.]

Now he stands in a place where nothing has ever existed, with borrowed Life Energy he doesn't understand and a support line he cannot afford to call even once.

[Note: The system wished him a pleasant day. Edward found this unhelpful.]

title: Monarch of Depravity
description:
Ian prayed to the Shadow Man with rats, a knife, and his own blood.

What showed up instead was a 25-year-old software engineer from the 21st century who had been buying eggs.

In a world where twelve gods are real and collecting, where cults carve devotion into living skin and priests of death walk around unhooking souls mid-conversation, Alden is, to put it plainly, the wrong man in the wrong body at the wrong time.

He handles it well, all things considered.

That part is true. Everything after is a little harder to explain.

[Note: The author has tried to present this story as faithfully as possible. Alden himself, when asked, remembers things differently. Both cannot be right.]

title: Mutation of the Apocalypse
description:
On the eve of his sixteenth birthday, a destitute orphan sat alone with a strawberry grape cake and called it a celebration.

Then every human on Earth heard the same voice at the same time.

"HUMANS OF EARTH! INITIATING EARTH MUTATION PROGRAM!"

Cars left the roads. Streets turned red. The world mutated. The fish, the dogs, the neighbors.

Lucas woke up the morning after his birthday to a holographic system informing him his agility was below the adult human average.

He had been an orphan fisherman since he was ten. He had survived on less.

"Never thought I'd say this, but I miss the sound of traffic jams."

[Note: First novel. The author welcomes feedback and cannot promise he won't cry. Chapters daily.]
